1→# Review: `deepmind/1100.lean` — Erdős Problem 1100
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    16→- [x] Starts with heading: `# Erdős Problem 1100`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1100`.
    18→- [x] Includes a plain-English description of all three parts of the problem.
    19→- [ ] *(Optional)* No bibliography entries for Erdős–Hall or Erdős–Simonovits papers. Acceptable since bibliography is optional.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1100 ... end Erdos1100`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1100`).
    25→- [x] `open Finset Real` appears **before** the namespace block (line 38).
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `sortedDivisors` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    30→- [x] `tauPerp` has a LaTeX docstring.
    31→- [x] No `noncomputable` needed — both definitions use computable operations (`Nat.divisors`, `Finset.sort`, `List.zip`, `List.filter`, `List.length`).
    32→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    33→- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase` (`sortedDivisors`, `tauPerp`).
    34→- [x] No problem-specific notation introduced (none needed; function names are clear).
    35→- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity-check declarations for the definitions. Minor — this is a "consider" item, not required.
    36→
    37→## 6. Theorem Statements
    38→
    39→- [x] All four declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
    40→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1100` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    41→- [x] All names follow `snake_case` conventions.
    42→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
    43→- [x] All four theorems have docstrings.
    44→- [x] Docstrings for Part 3 reference Erdős–Simonovits. Parts 1 and 2 reference "Erdős Problem 1100".
    45→
    46→### `answer()` usage
    47→
    48→**Part 1** — "Is it true that τ⊥(n)/ω(n) → ∞ for almost all n?" → yes/no question → **Case 1**.
    49→- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form.
    50→- [x] RHS formalizes the question as asked: for every M and ε > 0, the upper density of {n : τ⊥(n) ≤ M · ω(n)} is 0. This correctly captures "τ⊥(n)/ω(n) → ∞ for almost all n" (natural density formulation).
    51→
    52→**Part 2** — "Is it true that τ⊥(n) < exp((log n)^{o(1)})?" → yes/no question → **Case 1**.
    53→- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form.
    54→- [x] RHS correctly formalizes the o(1) exponent: ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N₀, ∀ n ≥ N₀, τ⊥(n) < exp((log n)^ε).
    55→
    56→**Part 3 (upper & lower)** — These are proved results (Erdős–Simonovits bounds), stated as direct assertions → **Case 3**.
    57→- [x] No `answer()` wrapper — direct assertion form.
    58→- [x] Upper bound: ∃ c > 0 such that for large k, every squarefree n with ω(n) = k has τ⊥(n) < (2 − c)^k. Matches the cited result.
    59→- [x] Lower bound: ∀ ε > 0, for large k, ∃ squarefree n with ω(n) = k and τ⊥(n) > (√2 − ε)^k. Correctly formalizes (√2 + o(1))^k < g(k).
    60→
    61→**Common rules:**
    62→- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters (Parts 1 and 2).
    63→
    64→## 7. Category Attribute
    65→
    66→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
    67→- [x] `erdos_1100`: `research open` — problem is open. ✓
    68→- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part2`: `research open` — problem is open. ✓
    69→- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_upper`: `research solved` — proved by Erdős–Simonovits. ✓
    70→- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_lower`: `research solved` — proved by Erdős–Simonovits. ✓
    71→- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.
    72→
    73→## 8. AMS Attribute
    74→
    75→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    76→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
    77→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (trivially — only one number).
    78→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    79→- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    80→
    81→AMS 11 (Number theory) is appropriate for this divisor-coprimality problem.
    82→
    83→## 9. Proof Bodies
    84→
    85→- [x] `erdos_1100`: uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form). ✓
    86→- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part2`: uses `by\n  sorry`. ✓
    87→- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_upper`: uses `by\n  sorry`. ✓
    88→- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_lower`: uses `by\n  sorry`. ✓
    89→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
    90→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open problems.
    91→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    92→
    93→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    94→
    95→- [x] Variants named `erdos_1100.variants.<descriptor>` (`part2`, `part3_upper`, `part3_lower`).
    96→- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
    97→- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
    98→- [x] All variants have docstrings explaining what they formalize.
    99→
   100→## 11. Code Quality
   101→
   102→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   103→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `→`, `↔`, `≥`, `≤`, `ε`.
   104→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   105→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
   106→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
   107→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   108→
   109→## 12. Build Verification
   110→
   111→- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`). No obvious issues anticipated; warnings from `sorry` in open problems are expected.
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→## Mathematical Accuracy
   116→
   117→- **Part 1:** The natural density formulation correctly captures "τ⊥(n)/ω(n) → ∞ for almost all n". Using `M : ℕ` rather than `M : ℝ` is equivalent (any real bound is dominated by some natural number).
   118→- **Part 2:** The ε-formulation correctly unpacks the o(1) exponent notation.
   119→- **Part 3 upper:** Directly formalizes g(k) < (2 − c)^k by universally quantifying over all squarefree n with ω(n) = k.
   120→- **Part 3 lower:** Correctly formalizes (√2 + o(1))^k < g(k) as: for every ε > 0 and large k, ∃ squarefree n with ω(n) = k achieving τ⊥(n) > (√2 − ε)^k. The encoding `(2 : ℝ) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / 2)` for √2 is valid.
   121→- **Definition correctness:** `sortedDivisors` sorts `Nat.divisors n` in ascending order; `tauPerp` counts consecutive coprime pairs via zip-with-tail and gcd filtering. Both correctly implement the mathematical definitions.
   122→
   123→## Verdict
   124→
   125→**PASS.** Clean, well-structured file. All checklist items satisfied. The three-part problem is correctly decomposed: Parts 1 and 2 as open yes/no questions with `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, and Part 3 split into separately proved upper and lower bounds as direct assertions with `research solved`. Mathematical formalization is accurate against the source.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1100.lean` — Erdős Problem 1100

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with heading: `# Erdős Problem 1100`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1100`.
- [x] Includes a plain-English description of all three parts of the problem.
- [ ] *(Optional)* No bibliography entries for Erdős–Hall or Erdős–Simonovits papers. Acceptable since bibliography is optional.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1100 ... end Erdos1100`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1100`).
- [x] `open Finset Real` appears **before** the namespace block (line 38).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `sortedDivisors` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `tauPerp` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed — both definitions use computable operations (`Nat.divisors`, `Finset.sort`, `List.zip`, `List.filter`, `List.length`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase` (`sortedDivisors`, `tauPerp`).
- [x] No problem-specific notation introduced (none needed; function names are clear).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity-check declarations for the definitions. Minor — this is a "consider" item, not required.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All four declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1100` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] All names follow `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] All four theorems have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings for Part 3 reference Erdős–Simonovits. Parts 1 and 2 reference "Erdős Problem 1100".

### `answer()` usage

**Part 1** — "Is it true that τ⊥(n)/ω(n) → ∞ for almost all n?" → yes/no question → **Case 1**.
- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form.
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as asked: for every M and ε > 0, the upper density of {n : τ⊥(n) ≤ M · ω(n)} is 0. This correctly captures "τ⊥(n)/ω(n) → ∞ for almost all n" (natural density formulation).

**Part 2** — "Is it true that τ⊥(n) < exp((log n)^{o(1)})?" → yes/no question → **Case 1**.
- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form.
- [x] RHS correctly formalizes the o(1) exponent: ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N₀, ∀ n ≥ N₀, τ⊥(n) < exp((log n)^ε).

**Part 3 (upper & lower)** — These are proved results (Erdős–Simonovits bounds), stated as direct assertions → **Case 3**.
- [x] No `answer()` wrapper — direct assertion form.
- [x] Upper bound: ∃ c > 0 such that for large k, every squarefree n with ω(n) = k has τ⊥(n) < (2 − c)^k. Matches the cited result.
- [x] Lower bound: ∀ ε > 0, for large k, ∃ squarefree n with ω(n) = k and τ⊥(n) > (√2 − ε)^k. Correctly formalizes (√2 + o(1))^k < g(k).

**Common rules:**
- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters (Parts 1 and 2).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] `erdos_1100`: `research open` — problem is open. ✓
- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part2`: `research open` — problem is open. ✓
- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_upper`: `research solved` — proved by Erdős–Simonovits. ✓
- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_lower`: `research solved` — proved by Erdős–Simonovits. ✓
- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (trivially — only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

AMS 11 (Number theory) is appropriate for this divisor-coprimality problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] `erdos_1100`: uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form). ✓
- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part2`: uses `by\n  sorry`. ✓
- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_upper`: uses `by\n  sorry`. ✓
- [x] `erdos_1100.variants.part3_lower`: uses `by\n  sorry`. ✓
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants named `erdos_1100.variants.<descriptor>` (`part2`, `part3_upper`, `part3_lower`).
- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] All variants have docstrings explaining what they formalize.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `→`, `↔`, `≥`, `≤`, `ε`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`). No obvious issues anticipated; warnings from `sorry` in open problems are expected.

---

## Mathematical Accuracy

- **Part 1:** The natural density formulation correctly captures "τ⊥(n)/ω(n) → ∞ for almost all n". Using `M : ℕ` rather than `M : ℝ` is equivalent (any real bound is dominated by some natural number).
- **Part 2:** The ε-formulation correctly unpacks the o(1) exponent notation.
- **Part 3 upper:** Directly formalizes g(k) < (2 − c)^k by universally quantifying over all squarefree n with ω(n) = k.
- **Part 3 lower:** Correctly formalizes (√2 + o(1))^k < g(k) as: for every ε > 0 and large k, ∃ squarefree n with ω(n) = k achieving τ⊥(n) > (√2 − ε)^k. The encoding `(2 : ℝ) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / 2)` for √2 is valid.
- **Definition correctness:** `sortedDivisors` sorts `Nat.divisors n` in ascending order; `tauPerp` counts consecutive coprime pairs via zip-with-tail and gcd filtering. Both correctly implement the mathematical definitions.

## Verdict

**PASS.** Clean, well-structured file. All checklist items satisfied. The three-part problem is correctly decomposed: Parts 1 and 2 as open yes/no questions with `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, and Part 3 split into separately proved upper and lower bounds as direct assertions with `research solved`. Mathematical formalization is accurate against the source.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
